news.headline: Indonesia considers significant reductions to nickel production to drive prices up. #mining #Indonesia #nickel #prices
U.S. AmCham China President optimistic about trade in China; discusses evolving relations.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Michael Hart, President of the American Chamber of Commerce in China (AmCham China), discussed the changing economic and trade dynamics between China and the United States in an interview with Beijing Review. Hart provided valuable perspectives on the opportunities arising from this evolving relationship.

news.headline: Castle Island Ventures' Nic Carter doubts US creating Bitcoin reserve; Matthew Sigel of VanEck calls it "Loserthink."
news.summary: Nic Carter, a Founding Partner at Castle Island Ventures, has raised doubts about the U.S. setting up a strategic Bitcoin reserve during the upcoming Trump administration, citing potential risks to the dollar's global dominance. This viewpoint has faced criticism from Matthew Sigel, Head of Digital Assets Research at VanEck, who strongly rebuked Carter's stance as "Loserthink" in a direct response on social media. In an interview with Bloomberg, Carter explained his support for Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) but expressed reluctance towards the U.S. government signaling a departure from its current monetary framework. He delved into the intricacies of defining a "strategic reserve," differentiating between simply retaining seized Bitcoin and actively procuring more assets.

news.headline: US stock futures rise after Fed rate cut; futures up for all major indices.10-year yield at 4.54%, 2-year at 4.32%. Market reacts to 'healthy' tariff news. GDP revisions and inflation data to be released soon. Stocks plunged in last session. Dow fell by over 1,100 points.
news.summary: The U.S. stock futures rose in premarket trading on Thursday after a previous decline following the Federal Reserve's hawkish stance. The 10-year Treasury note had a 4.54% yield, while the two-year note yielded 4.32%. The Fed cut interest rates by 0.25% to 4.25%-4.5%, signaling only two possible rate cuts in 2025. The probability of no interest rate change for the upcoming Jan. 31, 2025, decision was at 91.4%. GDP revisions were set to reveal if the economy maintained its estimated 2.8% growth rate. Key economic data, including the November PCE inflation index, was awaited. In premarket trading, major indices were up, with the S&P 500 ETF Trust rising 0.52%. In the previous session, the Dow Jones fell over 1,100 points, due to economic indicators like the current account deficit of $310.9 billion and declining housing starts. All S&P 500 sectors closed in the red, with consumer discretionary, real estate, and communication services seeing significant losses.

news.headline: Polyrizon appoints Dr. Michal Meir as Senior Director of Regulatory and Clinical Affairs preparing for a clinical trial for PL-14 in 2025.
news.summary: Raanana, Israel - December 19, 2024 - Polyrizon Ltd. (Nasdaq: PLRZ), a biotech company focusing on intranasal hydrogels, has named Dr. Michal Meir, a Senior Consultant at MedTech SME Ltd., as the new Senior Director of Regulatory and Clinical Affairs. Dr. Meir, with more than ten years of experience in medical devices and pharmaceuticals, will play a key role in preparing for the upcoming clinical trial of PL-14 scheduled for 2025. This strategic hire highlights Polyrizon's commitment to strong regulatory strategies, clinical management, and product development in the industry.

news.headline: Self-made billionaire Thomas Peterffy, 80, fled Hungary in 1965, founded Interactive Brokers, has a wealth of over $15 billion.
news.summary: Thomas Peterffy, aged 80, is not like many billionaires who inherit wealth; he is the self-made founder and chairman of Interactive Brokers. In 1965, Peterffy escaped Hungary and moved to the US. He has successfully built his fortune through hard work and dedication. This story showcases a unique journey of perseverance and success in the face of adversity.

news.headline: Microbix reported record product sales for Q4 and fiscal 2024, with revenues of $25.4 million and net earnings of $3.5 million. Antigen sales grew by 44% to $13.8 million, and QAPs by 38% to $7.0 million. Q4 revenue was $6.3 million, a 48% increase from Q4 2023. Gross margin percentage was 61% for 2024. Operating income for 2024 was $3.9 million, up from a loss in 2023. Cash provided by operating activities was $4.3 million, compared to cash used in operating activities in 2023.
news.summary: Microbix Biosystems Inc. reports record revenues for its fiscal year and fourth quarter ended September 30, 2024. In 2024, revenues were $25.4 million with a net income of $3.5 million, up significantly from $16.5 million in 2023. Sales of test ingredients and quality assessment products grew by 42% for the year. Antigen sales increased by 44% to $13.8 million, while QAP sales grew by 38% to $7.0 million. Gross margin percentage improved from 45% in 2023 to 61% in 2024. Operating income for 2024 was $3.9 million compared to a loss in 2023. Q4 revenue reached $6.3 million, a 48% increase from Q4 2023. Antigen revenues were up 50% and QAP revenues rose by 42%. Q4 gross margin percentage improved to 55% from 33% in 2023. Operating income for Q4 was $710,778, a significant improvement from a loss in Q4 2023. Cash provided by operating activities in 2024 was $4.3 million compared to a negative cash flow in 2023. Microbix ended 2024 with a current ratio of 7.15 and a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.35.

Clearside Biomedical's publication in Eye journal details retinal drug insights based on a presentation by Dr. Victor Chong.
news.summary: On December 19, 2024, Clearside Biomedical, Inc. (Nasdaq: CLSD) based in Alpharetta, Georgia, revealed that Eye, the official journal of The Royal College of Ophthalmologists, has released a comprehensive overview of key information regarding drug development and regulatory practices discussed during the prestigious Edridge Green Lecture. This lecture was conducted by Dr. Victor Chong, the Chief Medical Officer and Executive Vice President, Head of Research and Development at Clearside. The company specializes in enhancing the administration of treatments to the back of the eye via the suprachoroidal space (SCS®). This publication serves to highlight significant insights shared during the lecture for the benefit of the wider medical community.

news.headline: Kinaxis offers $40,000 award to support supply chain students at 3 universities - Texas Christian, University of Toronto, Carleton.
news.summary: In honor of its 40th anniversary, Kinaxis® (TSX:KXS), a prominent player in end-to-end supply chain orchestration, revealed a significant move in Ottawa, Ontario. news.headline: Aeva debuts 4D LiDAR at CES 2025, partnering with automotive leaders, showcasing new tech at event in Las Vegas.
news.summary: Aeva, a prominent company in advanced sensing and perception technology, revealed its involvement in CES 2025, scheduled from January 7th to 10th in Las Vegas. At the event, Aeva will present a cutting-edge long-range high-resolution 4D LiDAR sensor tailored for automotive use. Moreover, they will offer a sneak peek of Torc’s self-driving Freightliner Cascadia truck integrated with Aeva 4D LiDAR™ technology. Additionally, Aeva will highlight a fresh in-cabin LiDAR partnership. This initiative showcases Aeva's commitment to innovation and advancement in the sector.

news.headline: 908 Devices and Getinge partner to integrate bioreactors with MAVEN for automated control of glucose/lactate levels in cell cultures, enabling continuous real-time analysis without manual sampling. #bioprocessing #automation
news.summary: In an exciting collaboration, 908 Devices Inc. (Nasdaq: MASS) and Getinge have joined forces to integrate Getinge's bioreactors with 908 Devices' MAVEN. This partnership aims to automate the control of glucose and lactate levels in cell cultures. The integrated solution will allow scientists to monitor critical process parameters in bioreactors continuously and in real-time. This innovation eliminates the need for manual sampling, streamlining bioprocessing procedures. The collaboration seeks to enhance the efficiency and accuracy of monitoring glucose and lactate levels in cell cultures, benefiting the field of bioprocessing significantly.

news.headline: Traders now favour three BoE rate cuts in 2025.
Global industrial fat fraction market to reach $17.04 billion by 2034, with a projected 3% CAGR. Demand rising in various sectors.
news.summary: The global industrial fat fraction market is estimated to reach US$12.69 billion in 2024 and is projected to grow at a CAGR of 3% between 2024 and 2034. These fat fractions are increasingly used in various sectors such as food processing, cosmetics, medications, and industrial lubricants due to their nutritional benefits, stability, and adaptability to specific purposes. The demand for fat fractions as fuel substitutes is also rising with the expanding biodiesel industry. The market is expected to reach US$17.04 billion by 2034, with Japan holding a 4% share of the East Asian market by that time. The East Asian market for fat fractions is projected to grow at a CAGR of 3% from 2024 to 2034. Sales of fat fractions in Canada are also forecasted to increase. Overall, the market is expanding due to new applications and demand from fast-growing sectors in various countries.

news.headline: Graphite market to reach $36.40 billion by 2030 at 15.1% CAGR, driven by EV growth & green tech focus per MarketsandMarkets™.
news.summary: The Graphite Market is expected to increase from USD 15.67 billion in 2024 to USD 36.40 billion by 2030, with a CAGR of 15.1%. This growth is attributed to the rising demand for graphite in energy storage technologies, particularly lithium-ion batteries used in electric vehicles and renewable energy sectors. Emerging battery technologies like solid-state and sodium-ion batteries further contribute to the demand for graphite. Industries such as semiconductors, electronics, refractories, steel production, and aerospace also rely on graphite. Sustainability initiatives and graphite's properties like thermal stability and conductivity enhance its importance across various sectors. Key players include companies like AMG, SGL Carbon, and Imerys. Asia Pacific is a significant market region for graphite growth. Key drivers include the increasing production of electric vehicles and renewable energy storage systems, while challenges include finding potential substitutes like carbon nanomaterials. The synthetic graphite segment is projected to have the fastest growth by 2030, with electrode applications being the largest in the market in 2023, and high-purity graphite dominating market share in the same year.
